##NAMES=Palermoite
##RRUFFID=R120141
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Li_2_SrAl_4_(PO_4_)_4_(OH)_4_
##LOCALITY=Palermo No. 1 mine, Groton, New Hampshire, USA
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Benjamin N. Schumer
##DESCRIPTION=Striated, colorless, elongated prismatic crystals, associated with quartz, radiating childrenite, natropalermoite (R130092), and lefontite (R140539).
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R120141
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Li_1.87_Na_0.14_)(Sr_0.79_Ca_0.21_)(Al_3.89_Fe_0.01_)(PO_4_)_4.06_(OH)_3.99_
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 11.577 b: 15.879 c: 7.3276 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 1347 crystal system: orthorhombic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
